calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Modifying Trust
Off Calendar Sep-05-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Gladden appeared by telephone for Julie Van Alyea.
Matt Cappola (Guardian Ad Litem) appeared by telephone.

In response to the Court's questions Mr. Cappola stated that he works in wealth management, that Julie has three Daughters, that the Trust has grown so much there are enough funds for each Child to buy a home, that the concern is that at a younger age there is a higher probability of using those funds, and that when you have Children of your own, you use funds to provide for your family. Mr. Cappola also stated that he is not suggesting that these Children are less responsible at age 35, that they are protecting the wealth for the next generation, that this is a decision Julie has made and that he has known Julie since before she had her Children. In addition, Mr. Cappola stated that this has to do with the growth of the Trust and to distribute wealth a little bit further, that the Children will make wiser decisions at age 43, that he knows the family and the Children really well, that he feels the Children are going to be just fine with distribution as set up, that he fully supports larger distribution at a later time and that there is wealth in the family. The Petition for Order Modifying Trust is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Order Modifying Trust
Off Calendar Aug-15-2017 Continued To Sep-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Gladden for Petitioner.

The Court noted that there is no appearance by the Guardian Ad Litem ("GAD") or any others. Mr. Gladden stated that he has a consent to the petition and the amendment from the GAD. The Court stated that there is nothing in this to the benefit the Minor Children, that it would like to ask the GAD some questions, that all this does is ultimately delay access to Children from age 35 to age 43 and that it would like the GAD to come to Court. Mr. Gladden stated that the parents feel the age should be older based on how the Children are growing up and that he can provide a declaration. The Petition for Order Modifying Trust is continued to September 5, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
